---
title: 1D Features
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    number-sections: true
authors: "Marta Aransay, Carlota de la Concepción, Marta Rodrigo, Lucía Santos"<
date: 10/03/2026
---

# 1D Features

## Protein 1

The **** protein has *** secondary structure

## Protein 2

The *** protein has---

## Protein 3

The primary sequence of MoHog1 (351 aa) exhibits the classic architecture of a MAP kinase. The catalytic domain (residues 23-311) contains all the conserved motifs necessary for phosphotransferase activity. Notably, the activation loop features the TGY motif (T171/Y173), characteristic of the stress-activated protein kinase (SAPK) family. Key residues for ATP binding and catalysis were identified at positions K52 (VAK motif) and D149 (HRDLK motif). Furthermore, the C-terminal region contains a docking site (CD domain) with conserved aspartic acids (D309/D312) predicted to mediate interactions with regulatory partners and substrates
